automotive-ev-battery.title: Volvo Trucks increases range of flagship e-truck to 700 km
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volvo Trucks has increased the range of its new long-haul electric truck, the FH Aero Electric, from the previously stated 600 kilometers to up to 700 kilometers. The Swedish manufacturer will officially present the vehicle in June, marking a significant advancement in heavy-duty electric vehicle capability.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Engie Vianeo to install over 4,500 AC charging points in Belgium
automotive-ev-battery.summary: French charging infrastructure provider Engie Vianeo has secured contracts to deploy over 4,500 charging points across Belgium. The projects include nearly 3,000 22-kW chargers in Wallonia and 1,640 additional points in the Brussels Capital Region, significantly expanding Belgium's public charging network.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Australia's NSW launches $100m EV strategy
automotive-ev-battery.summary: New South Wales has unveiled a A$100 million electric vehicle strategy focused on expanding charging infrastructure, supporting electric trucks, and workforce development. The program aims to accelerate EV adoption across regional areas while reducing operational costs for vehicle operators.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Vilsa delivers its mineral water with three eActros 600 battery-electric trucks
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mineral water company Vilsa has deployed three Mercedes-Benz eActros 600 heavy-duty electric trucks for long-distance transport operations. The vehicles are supported by three in-house charging stations and will operate in a two-shift system, demonstrating commercial viability of battery-electric trucks for logistics.

automotive-ev-battery.title: ZF presents e-bus drivetrain health check tool
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Supplier ZF unveiled new software for monitoring and predictive maintenance of electric bus drivetrains at the Bus2Bus trade fair in Berlin. The system will initially support the electric ZF portal axle AxTrax 2 LF, helping fleet operators optimize maintenance and reduce downtime.

automotive-ev-battery.title: GAC Aion UT launches in Europe from €27,990
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese automaker GAC has announced the European market launch of the Aion UT electric vehicle, priced from €27,990. The model will begin sales this quarter in Finland, Greece, Poland, and Portugal, with additional European markets following in the third quarter.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Temsa delivers 15 electric buses to Lithuania
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Turkish bus manufacturer Temsa has delivered 15 electric MD9 electriCITY buses to Lithuanian operator UAB Kautra for deployment in Kaunas and municipalities across the country. The order represents continued adoption of battery-electric buses in public transportation in Eastern Europe.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Leaked: Car Industry's Latest Demands Could Cost EU Extra €74 Billion In Oil Imports
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A leaked document reveals the European automotive industry is seeking weaker CO2 emission targets, which could result in an additional €74 billion in annual oil imports for the EU. The demands directly conflict with growing consumer interest in affordable electric vehicle models.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Over 200 New EV Chargers Coming To Ohio
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Ohio is deploying over 200 new electric vehicle chargers through a combination of $51 million in federal NEVI funding and at least $26 million in private sector investment. The expansion will establish 64 new fast-charging stations across the state.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Only 6 Auto Brands Had EV Sales Growth In USA In 1st Quarter 2026
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Analysis of first quarter 2026 US electric vehicle sales reveals that only six of more than two dozen automotive brands achieved year-over-year EV sales growth, indicating a significant market slowdown. The data reflects broader challenges in the domestic EV market.

science-space.title: NASA's Webb Redefines Dividing Line Between Planets, Stars
science-space.summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has challenged conventional planetary formation theory by examining the object 29 Cygni b, which raises new questions about how massive planets form. The discovery suggests that bottom-up accretion models may not fully explain the formation of heavier planetary bodies, potentially redefining the classification boundary between planets and stars.

science-space.title: NASA Finds Young Stars Dim in X-rays Surprisingly Quickly
science-space.summary: A new study using NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory reveals that young stars similar to our Sun reduce their X-ray output and calm down faster than previously believed. The findings, published in The Astrophysical Journal, provide new insights into stellar evolution and the aging process of sun-like stars.

science-space.title: NASA's Artemis 2 Moonshot Was Just the 'Opening Act' for America's Return to the Moon
science-space.summary: NASA leadership has characterized the successful Artemis 2 mission as the beginning of a lunar "relay race" designed to establish a moon base and eventually enable human missions to Mars. The agency's ambitious multi-mission strategy underscores America's long-term commitment to sustained lunar exploration and deep space exploration.

science-space.title: The Moon's Oldest and Darkest Craters Could Be Hiding the Most Water Ice
science-space.summary: Recent research indicates that permanently shadowed craters near the Moon's south pole are likely to contain significant deposits of water ice, offering crucial resources for future lunar missions. These findings have important implications for establishing sustainable human settlements and supporting long-term lunar exploration objectives.

science-space.title: Nutrition Research Arrives Aboard Space Station
science-space.summary: NASA has begun conducting nutrition research aboard the International Space Station to prepare for long-duration missions to the Moon, Mars, and beyond. The research focuses on developing sustainable methods for growing plants and cultivating alternative food sources such as algae to sustain future deep space exploration crews.

science-space.title: Artemis 2 Astronauts Call ISS: Record-Breaking Earth-Moon Distance Communication
science-space.summary: Astronauts aboard the Artemis 2 mission successfully conducted the farthest crew-to-crew call ever made in space by communicating with colleagues aboard the International Space Station across the Earth-Moon distance. This historic communication milestone demonstrates advanced technological capabilities essential for supporting future deep space exploration missions.

science-space.title: Scientists Use Rare 'Einstein Cross' to Learn About Young Galaxy With Surprisingly Old Stars
science-space.summary: Astronomers have utilized the rare gravitational lensing phenomenon known as the 'Einstein Cross' to study an exceptionally distant young galaxy containing unexpectedly mature stars. The discovery enables unprecedented analysis of stellar composition and evolution during the early universe, challenging existing models of galactic development.

science-space.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4859-4866: One Small Crater and Thousands of Polygons
science-space.summary: NASA's Curiosity rover spent the past week driving towards and investigating a small crater approximately 10 meters in diameter, informally named 'Antofagasta' after a Chilean city and region. The rover's continued exploration contributes valuable geological data to understanding Mars' surface composition and history.

science-space.title: A Hug for Home Away from Home
science-space.summary: NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, along with Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen, successfully splashed down in the Pacific Ocean off the California coast following the completion of the Artemis II mission. The crew's safe return marks a significant milestone in NASA's ambitious lunar exploration program.

science-space.title: The Lyrids Are Coming: How to Watch Meteor Showers From the Middle of a City
science-space.summary: As the Lyrids meteor shower approaches, astronomers offer practical guidance for urban observers seeking to photograph and observe meteors despite light pollution challenges. With appropriate timing, camera placement, and patience, city dwellers can successfully capture compelling meteor shower imagery and participate in this seasonal astronomical event.

tech-ai.title: AI data center startup Fluidstack in talks for $1B round at $18B valuation months after hitting $7.5B, says report
tech-ai.summary: Fluidstack, an AI data center startup, is reportedly in advanced fundraising discussions for a $1 billion round that would value the company at $18 billion. The development follows the company's $50 billion infrastructure deal with Anthropic, underscoring continued investor appetite for AI computing infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: Someone planted backdoors in dozens of WordPress plug-ins used in thousands of websites
tech-ai.summary: Dozens of WordPress plugins were compromised with malware after being sold to a new corporate owner, exposing thousands of websites to security risks. The incident highlights the vulnerability of popular open-source plugins when ownership changes hands.

tech-ai.title: Max Hodak's Science Corp. is preparing to place its first sensor in a human brain
tech-ai.summary: Science Corp is advancing toward implanting its first brain sensor in humans, with potential applications in treating neurological conditions through electrical stimulation therapy. The device represents a significant milestone in neurotechnology and could offer new therapeutic options for brain and spinal cord damage.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark confirmed that the company has maintained engagement with the U.S. government regarding its AI initiatives, even as the company pursues litigation against federal agencies. The briefing underscores ongoing dialogue between major AI developers and government officials on AI policy matters.

tech-ai.title: London gets closer to its first robotaxi service as Waymo begins testing
tech-ai.summary: Waymo has begun testing its autonomous vehicle service in London, with potential for commercial deployment pending government approval. The expansion marks a significant step toward bringing robotaxi services to the United Kingdom's transportation market.

tech-ai.title: Google adds AI Skills to Chrome to help you save favorite workflows
tech-ai.summary: Google is introducing 'Skills' functionality to Chrome, enabling users to save and reuse AI prompts and workflows across websites through Gemini integration. The feature aims to streamline repetitive tasks and improve browser-based AI utility for users.

tech-ai.title: Privacy advocates want Google to stop handing consumer data over to ICE
tech-ai.summary: The Electronic Frontier Foundation is petitioning California and New York attorneys general to investigate Google for allegedly providing user data to immigration enforcement without notification. The action challenges Google's stated commitment to inform users before disclosing personal information to law enforcement agencies.

tech-ai.title: Elon Musk grilled by senator over X Money plans
tech-ai.summary: Senator Elizabeth Warren has written to Elon Musk expressing concerns about the security and systemic risks of his planned X Money payments platform, citing concerns about consumer protection and financial stability. Musk has indicated the platform will enter early public access in April.

tech-ai.title: Financial risk management platform Pillar raises $20M seed in round led by a16z
tech-ai.summary: Pillar, a financial risk management platform, has secured $20 million in seed funding led by Andreessen Horowitz to democratize institutional-grade hedging tools for small and medium-sized enterprises. The company aims to make sophisticated risk management accessible to businesses previously unable to access such capabilities.

tech-ai.title: The attacks on Sam Altman are a warning for the AI world
tech-ai.summary: Recent violent incidents targeting OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and other officials, motivated by AI safety concerns and data center opposition, underscore growing tensions between AI development and public concerns about risks. The attacks highlight emerging challenges around AI industry expansion and societal anxieties about technological development.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Trump says war 'close to over', Israel pounds Lebanon
world-news.summary: US President Trump indicated that a second round of negotiations could be held in Pakistan as the military conflict between Israel and Iran-backed forces continues. The statement comes amid ongoing Israeli strikes on Lebanon and a continued US naval blockade of Iranian ports.

world-news.title: Israel and Lebanon hold rare talks in Washington, DC, amid Iran war
world-news.summary: Israel and Lebanon held their first direct talks in decades in Washington, DC, marking a significant diplomatic development in the Middle East conflict. US officials indicated that additional time will be needed to reach comprehensive agreements between the two nations.

world-news.title: US blockade of Iran ports irresponsible and dangerous, China says
world-news.summary: China, the world's largest buyer of Iranian oil, condemned the US naval blockade of Iranian ports as destabilizing to regional security. Beijing stated that the blockade undermines the fragile ceasefire in the Middle East conflict.

world-news.title: Hamas rejects Gaza disarmament plan, Palestinian official says
world-news.summary: A Palestinian official stated that Hamas will not proceed with ceasefire negotiations until Israel fully meets its previously agreed commitments. The rejection represents a significant obstacle to ongoing peace talks regarding the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.

world-news.title: Israeli attacks kill 11, including two children, in day of strikes on Gaza
world-news.summary: Israeli military strikes on northern Gaza resulted in at least 11 deaths, including a three-year-old child and a 14-year-old, escalating civilian casualties in the ongoing conflict. The strikes represent the latest in a series of intensive military operations in the Gaza Strip.

world-news.title: JD Vance defends backing 'great guy' Orbán's campaign after landslide defeat
world-news.summary: US Vice President JD Vance reaffirmed support for Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán following Orbán's electoral defeat, stating he could work effectively with Orbán's successor Péter Magyar. The statement reflects the Trump administration's continued engagement with Hungary's political leadership.

world-news.title: Delcy Rodriguez calls for a 'Venezuela free of sanctions' amid US detente
world-news.summary: Venezuela's government called for comprehensive sanctions relief, arguing that limited relief measures have been insufficient to address the country's economic crisis. The statement comes as part of broader diplomatic engagement between Caracas and Washington.

world-news.title: The Take: Who is keeping the fight alive in Sudan's war?
world-news.summary: A documentary analysis examines the ongoing dynamics of Sudan's three-year conflict and the various actors continuing to fuel the civil war. The report provides insight into the humanitarian and geopolitical dimensions of the prolonged conflict.

world-news.title: French police arrest students protesting anti-Semitism law
world-news.summary: French police arrested university students in Paris during a sit-in opposing a controversial anti-Semitism bill, reflecting domestic debate over free speech and religious protection laws. The arrests highlight tension between civil liberties and efforts to combat discrimination in France.

world-news.title: 'They are striking everywhere' - Paramedics in Lebanon speak to BBC after Israeli strikes
world-news.summary: BBC Middle East correspondent reported from Nabatieh, Lebanon, documenting the humanitarian impact of Israeli military operations on civilian infrastructure and emergency services. The report highlights the deteriorating conditions in areas affected by cross-border strikes.

finance-markets.title: Strait of Hormuz tankers stop or turn around amid US blockade
finance-markets.summary: US military operations have successfully halted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz in an effort to seal off Iranian ports, with no vessels passing through in the first 24 hours of the blockade. The action represents a significant disruption to global energy markets and represents a major escalation in US-Iran tensions.

finance-markets.title: Wall Street banks break records as Iran war drives trading boom
finance-markets.summary: Major US financial institutions including JPMorgan Chase, Citigroup and Wells Fargo reported combined profits exceeding $25 billion in the first quarter, buoyed by heightened trading activity generated by Middle East tensions. The surge in volatility and market uncertainty has created substantial revenue opportunities for global banking operations.

finance-markets.title: Trump floats new US-Iran talks within days
finance-markets.summary: President Trump has signaled willingness to engage in direct negotiations with Iran, with preliminary discussions potentially occurring this week in Islamabad and further talks planned in Europe. The diplomatic overture suggests a potential de-escalation pathway amid the ongoing military confrontation.

finance-markets.title: HSBC Private Bank Cuts India Stocks, Adds Gold on Iran War Risks
finance-markets.summary: HSBC Private Bank has downgraded emerging Asian equities and reduced India stock exposure while increasing allocations to gold, cash and hedge funds in response to Iran conflict risks and oil price volatility. The strategic repositioning reflects institutional concerns about energy supply shocks and geopolitical uncertainty.

finance-markets.title: BlackRock profits jump as it draws in $130bn
finance-markets.summary: Asset management giant BlackRock reported significantly increased earnings in the first quarter while attracting $130 billion in new capital inflows, driven by demand for higher-fee investment products. The strong results underscore continued investor appetite for professional asset management amid market turbulence.

finance-markets.title: HSBC's Liao: Customers in Risk‑Off Mode as Tensions Rise
finance-markets.summary: HSBC's Asia and Middle East Co-CEO David Liao reported that the bank's customer base has shifted into risk-averse positioning amid Iran conflict escalation, though underlying balance sheets remain stable across wholesale and retail divisions. The shift indicates defensive investor sentiment while financial fundamentals remain resilient.

finance-markets.title: Chinese Shares Erase Iran War Losses on Economic Resilience
finance-markets.summary: China's onshore equity markets have recovered losses sustained since the outbreak of Iran-related conflict, outperforming broader Asian indices through demonstrations of economic durability and insulation policies against oil supply disruptions. The rebound suggests investor confidence in China's economic fundamentals despite geopolitical headwinds.

finance-markets.title: China shock 2.0: the flood of high-tech goods that will change the world
finance-markets.summary: Chinese companies are dramatically expanding market share in advanced technology sectors through aggressive competition, substantial government subsidies and manufacturing scale advantages, reshaping global high-tech industries. The technological export surge represents a significant shift in global competitive dynamics across multiple advanced sectors.

finance-markets.title: Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh reveals assets worth more than $130mn
finance-markets.summary: Trump's nominee to lead the Federal Reserve, Kevin Warsh, disclosed personal assets exceeding $130 million, including $10 million in consulting fees from a prominent family office. The disclosure provides insight into potential conflicts of interest and financial relationships ahead of his confirmation process.

finance-markets.title: China's Aluminum Exports to Surge as Mideast Disruptions Persist
finance-markets.summary: Chinese aluminum exports are expected to increase substantially in coming months as international buyers seek alternative sources to compensate for supply disruptions in the Persian Gulf region. The anticipated surge represents a significant commercial opportunity for Chinese producers amid global supply chain realignment.

health-biotech.title: Pharmalittle: We're reading about an FDA push for trial transparency, a Novo-OpenAI deal, and more
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has notified over 2,200 companies and researchers of mandatory requirements to report clinical trial results, with potential fines for non-compliance. This regulatory action underscores the agency's intensified focus on transparency and accountability in drug development.

health-biotech.title: Updated: FDA asks for data on Lilly's Foundayo to assess heart, liver risks
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has requested additional safety data from Eli Lilly regarding Foundayo, the newly approved obesity medication, to evaluate potential cardiovascular and hepatic risks. This follow-up review reflects ongoing regulatory scrutiny of the drug's safety profile post-approval.

health-biotech.title: Maryland state affordability board sets its first price cap for a medicine
health-biotech.summary: Maryland's drug affordability board has established its initial price cap for Jardiance, a type 2 diabetes medication, with implementation targeted for January 2027. This regulatory action represents a significant milestone in state-level pharmaceutical cost management.

health-biotech.title: Eli Lilly to pay up to $300M for cancer biotech CrossBridge Bio
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly has agreed to acquire antibody-drug conjugate startup CrossBridge Bio for up to $300 million, expanding its oncology portfolio. The acquisition reflects continued industry consolidation and Lilly's strategic investment in targeted cancer therapies.

health-biotech.title: FDA approves Travere's Filspari as first drug for the kidney disease FSGS
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has expanded Filspari's label to approve the drug as the first therapy for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS), a serious kidney condition. This approval represents a significant therapeutic advance for patients with limited treatment options.

health-biotech.title: For Ben Sasse, Revolution Medicines' pancreatic cancer trial felt like his best, only option
health-biotech.summary: Former Senator Ben Sasse participated in Revolution Medicines' clinical trial for a pancreatic cancer drug, providing a firsthand account of experimental therapy participation. The story highlights both the potential and challenges of engaging in early-stage drug development for serious malignancies.

health-biotech.title: Parker Institute doubles down on cancer vaccines as part of ongoing reboot
health-biotech.summary: The Parker Institute for Cancer Immunotherapy is increasing its commitment to mRNA cancer vaccine development despite industry hesitancy around the technology. The investment signals confidence in mRNA's potential for oncology applications beyond infectious disease prevention.

health-biotech.title: Revolution Medicines leads latest wave of stock offerings, totaling $1.5B
health-biotech.summary: Revolution Medicines led a biotech capital raise surge on Monday with multiple companies including Spyre Therapeutics and Allogene Therapeutics conducting stock offerings totaling $1.5 billion. The offerings reflect continued investor confidence in the biotechnology sector despite broader market uncertainties.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Dana-Farber CEO talks untangling from Mass General Brigham and building new cancer hospital
health-biotech.summary: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute CEO Benjamin Ebert outlined plans for the institution's separation from Mass General Brigham and construction of a new dedicated cancer hospital. The strategic initiative reflects the organization's commitment to cancer care independence and modernization.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Congress returns to a packed health care agenda
health-biotech.summary: Congress is returning from recess to address multiple significant healthcare policy issues on its legislative agenda. Key healthcare matters are expected to receive increased attention as lawmakers resume their sessions.

startups-vc.title: North America Q1 Funding Surges Across Stages To Record Level
startups-vc.summary: U.S. and Canadian companies secured $252.6 billion in seed through growth-stage funding during Q1 2026, more than three times the prior quarter and the largest quarterly total on record. The surge reflects robust investor appetite across all funding stages in North America's startup ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: China Leads Asia's Startup Funding To Its Highest Level In More Than 3 Years
startups-vc.summary: Asian startup funding reached $27.4 billion in Q1 2026, up 20% from the prior quarter and nearly double year-ago levels, marking the region's highest funding level in over three years. China led the growth, demonstrating strong investor confidence in the region's entrepreneurial ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: AI Drives Europe's Second Straight Quarter Of Funding Gain As Deal Volume Falls Sharply
startups-vc.summary: European venture funding reached $17.6 billion in Q1 2026, up nearly 30% year-over-year and marking the second consecutive quarter of growth, with AI accounting for over 50% of total funding for the first time. The trend reflects a concentrated investment shift toward artificial intelligence across the continent.

startups-vc.title: AI data center startup Fluidstack in talks for $1B round at $18B valuation months after hitting $7.5B, says report
startups-vc.summary: Fluidstack, an AI data center startup, is reportedly in discussions to raise $1 billion at a $18 billion valuation, representing significant growth since its previous $7.5 billion valuation. The funding follows a reported $50 billion deal with Anthropic to build data center infrastructure.

startups-vc.title: Anthropic Only Has ~5,000 Employees. Almost No One Has Ever Been This Efficient. That's By Choice.
startups-vc.summary: Anthropic has achieved $30 billion in annualized revenue with only 3,000 to 5,000 employees, representing the fastest revenue scale and leanest headcount ratio in technology history. The company's efficiency model highlights an unconventional approach to scaling a generative AI powerhouse.

startups-vc.title: Slate Auto raises $650M to fund its affordable EV truck plans
startups-vc.summary: Slate Auto secured $650 million in its latest funding round led by existing investor TWG Global, a firm managed by Los Angeles Dodgers owner Mark Walter. The capital will support the development and commercialization of affordable electric vehicle trucks.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Largest Financings Went To Defense, Wearables, Energy And Security
startups-vc.summary: This week's largest funding rounds demonstrated investor diversification across multiple sectors, with Saronic, an Austin-based autonomous vessel developer, leading with a $1.75 billion Series D. Defense, wearables, energy, and security sectors attracted significant capital during the period.

startups-vc.title: Fintech Startups Globally Raise More Money In Far Fewer Deals In Q1 2026
startups-vc.summary: Global fintech startups raised $12 billion across 751 deals in Q1 2026, a 5% increase in capital compared to the same period last year despite a 31% decline in deal count. The trend indicates larger, more selective funding rounds concentrated among stronger fintech companies.

startups-vc.title: Financial risk management platform Pillar raises $20M seed in round led by a16z
startups-vc.summary: Pillar, a financial risk management platform, closed a $20 million seed funding round led by Andreessen Horowitz to democratize institutional-grade hedging tools for small and medium-sized enterprises. The company aims to make sophisticated financial hedging as accessible as standard software applications.

startups-vc.title: Announcing the YC AI Stack
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has partnered with nearly two dozen technology companies to offer students attending YC events access to over $25,000 in complimentary credits for AI development tools. The initiative aims to reduce barriers to entry for aspiring founders building with artificial intelligence.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released critical updates addressing 167 security vulnerabilities across Windows and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and the actively exploited "BlueHammer" flaw in Windows Defender. Concurrently, Google Chrome patched its fourth zero-day of 2026, and Adobe Reader released an emergency update for a remote code execution vulnerability under active attack.

cybersecurity.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
cybersecurity.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in legacy Internet routers to harvest authentication tokens from over 18,000 Microsoft Office networks without deploying malware. The campaign represents a sophisticated state-sponsored espionage effort targeting enterprise authentication infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
cybersecurity.summary: An Iran-linked hacktivist group claimed responsibility for a destructive data-wiping attack against Stryker, a major medical technology company, forcing the firm to send home over 5,000 employees at its Ireland headquarters. The incident highlights the vulnerability of critical healthcare infrastructure to state-sponsored cyber operations.

cybersecurity.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: U.S. and international authorities dismantled four major IoT botnets—Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad—that compromised over three million devices and conducted record-breaking distributed denial-of-service attacks. The coordinated disruption effort involved law enforcement from the U.S., Canada, and Germany targeting the infrastructure behind these highly disruptive campaigns.

cybersecurity.title: 'Starkiller' Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
cybersecurity.summary: A sophisticated phishing-as-a-service platform called Starkiller uses disguised links to load legitimate target websites while acting as a relay to intercept credentials and multi-factor authentication tokens. The service circumvents traditional anti-phishing defenses by hosting real login infrastructure rather than static replicas.

cybersecurity.title: Germany Doxes "UNKN," Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
cybersecurity.summary: German authorities identified 31-year-old Russian national Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin as "UNKN," the elusive leader of notorious ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil. Shchukin is linked to at least 130 cybercrime and extortion operations against victims across Germany between 2019 and 2021.

cybersecurity.title: ShowDoc RCE Flaw CVE-2025-0520 Actively Exploited on Unpatched Servers
cybersecurity.summary: A critical remote code execution vulnerability in ShowDoc, a document management platform widely used in China, is experiencing active exploitation in the wild with a CVSS score of 9.4. The flaw stems from improper validation of unrestricted file uploads, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary commands on vulnerable systems.

cybersecurity.title: CISA Adds 6 Known Exploited Flaws in Fortinet, Microsoft, and Adobe Software
cybersecurity.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency added six vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including a critical SQL injection flaw in Fortinet FortiClient EMS with a CVSS score of 9.1. The additions reflect confirmed active exploitation of critical infrastructure software.

cybersecurity.title: 108 Malicious Chrome Extensions Steal Google and Telegram Data, Affecting 20,000 Users
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers discovered a coordinated campaign of 108 malicious Chrome extensions communicating with shared command-and-control infrastructure to harvest credentials from Google and Telegram users. The extensions also inject advertisements and arbitrary JavaScript code into web pages, affecting approximately 20,000 users.

cybersecurity.title: Mirax Android RAT Turns Devices into SOCKS5 Proxies, Reaching 220,000 via Meta Ads
cybersecurity.summary: The Mirax remote access trojan is actively targeting Spanish-speaking regions with campaigns reaching over 220,000 accounts through Meta platform advertisements. The malware enables threat actors to convert compromised Android devices into SOCKS5 proxies and establish full remote control over affected devices.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin's $76,000 breakout fails but a rare signal is hinting at major market bottom
crypto-web3.summary: Derivatives funding rates have now remained negative for 46 days, a streak last seen following the FTX crash which marked the bottom of 2022's crypto winter.

crypto-web3.title: The next Fed chair has crypto exposure in his portfolio. Here are the details
crypto-web3.summary: Kevin Warsh's financial disclosure reveals stakes in DeFi protocols, Ethereum scaling networks, a Bitcoin Lightning startup, and prediction markets — all of which he's promised to sell.

crypto-web3.title: Rakuten to allow XRP to be used as payment method by its 44 million customers
crypto-web3.summary: Rakuten Pay users will also be able to spot trade XRP via the Rakuten Pay app and exchange the Japanese e-commerce giant’s points to purchase Ripple’s token

crypto-web3.title: Popular DeFi platform warns users to stay away from its site after security breach
crypto-web3.summary: The team that helps operate the platform, CoW Swap, said that it was working to resolve the issue for the DEX aggregator.

crypto-web3.title: Ethereum Foundation unveils $1M audit subsidy program to boost crypto security and cut costs for builders
crypto-web3.summary: The new initiative aims to address a persistent challenge in crypto development—the high cost of smart contract security audits.

crypto-web3.title: Goldman Sachs files for bitcoin income ETF in crypto push
crypto-web3.summary: The bank is moving deeper into crypto with a bitcoin ETF that generates income by selling options on bitcoin-linked funds, following BlackRock’s push into similar yield-focused products.

crypto-web3.title: High Roller stock more than doubles on prediction markets partnership with Crypto.com
crypto-web3.summary: The announcement comes as an increasing number of companies move into prediction markets, an industry that is estimated to grow to $1 trillion by 2030

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin climbs to highest level since Feb. 5 crash that sent price plunging to $60,000
crypto-web3.summary: Optimism over developments in the Middle East sparked a sharp decline in oil prices and rallies across risk markets.

crypto-web3.title: Why Cantor Fitzgerald thinks Robinhood and Coinbase are the best ways to play the prediction market boom
crypto-web3.summary: Cantor Fitzgerald says Robinhood and Coinbase are best positioned to dominate the prediction market space by leveraging their massive retail scale and existing trading infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Scroll moves to cut costs after top protocol migrates to Optimism
crypto-web3.summary: The downsizing follows a financial hit after Scroll's top protocol migrated to Optimism, draining nearly $160 million in TVL and $13 million in annualized fees.

energy-climate.title: Iran war analysis: How 60 nations have responded to the global energy crisis
energy-climate.summary: One month into the US and Israel's war on Iran, at least 60 countries have responded to significant disruptions in global energy markets. The article analyzes international responses to what represents a major geopolitical threat to energy security and climate transition efforts.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 10 April 2026: Worst energy crisis 'ever' | India withdraws COP33 bid | Drag artists and climate change
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly digest covers major developments including what is being characterized as the worst energy crisis to date and India's withdrawal from hosting COP33. The roundup addresses critical climate and energy policy developments affecting international negotiations and renewable energy transitions.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: Record wind and solar saved UK from gas imports worth £1bn in March 2026
energy-climate.summary: Record-breaking wind and solar generation in the UK during March 2026 eliminated the need for approximately £1 billion in gas imports. The analysis demonstrates the economic and strategic value of renewable energy expansion in reducing energy dependency and import costs.

energy-climate.title: IESO signs 20-year contracts for 14 renewable energy projects
energy-climate.summary: Ontario's Independent Electricity System Operator has secured 20-year agreements with 14 renewable energy projects through competitive bidding, committing to over 3 TWh of annual electricity supply. This significant long-term investment strengthens Ontario's renewable energy infrastructure and decarbonization trajectory.

energy-climate.title: Marine heatwaves 'nearly double' the economic damage caused by tropical cyclones
energy-climate.summary: Research reveals that tropical cyclones intensifying over marine heatwaves become significantly more destructive, with economic damages nearly doubling compared to standard cyclones. This finding underscores the compounding climate impacts of warming ocean temperatures on extreme weather events and coastal economies.

energy-climate.title: North America's energy transition is accelerating, but policy volatility is reshaping the winners
energy-climate.summary: Renewable energy is poised to expand its share of North America's power mix substantially by the mid-2030s, yet uncertainty from shifting policy environments is creating unpredictability in the energy transition outcomes. The analysis highlights the tension between accelerating renewable deployment and political instability affecting energy markets.

energy-climate.title: Dominican Republic opens bids for 600 MW renewables tender
energy-climate.summary: The Dominican Republic has launched competitive bidding for 600 MW of wind and solar projects with mandatory integrated battery storage systems. This procurement initiative represents a substantial investment in renewable energy infrastructure while prioritizing grid stability through energy storage integration.

energy-climate.title: Mexico updates distributed generation rules to formally integrate energy storage
energy-climate.summary: Mexico's energy regulator has published new provisions updating technical and administrative criteria for distributed generation systems with formal energy storage integration. The regulatory update addresses technical standards, installed capacity limits, and interconnection procedures to support grid modernization.

energy-climate.title: XGS Energy, CC Power sign 115MW geothermal development agreement
energy-climate.summary: California Community Power and XGS Energy have entered into a development agreement for 115 MW of geothermal energy capacity in California. This partnership expands California's renewable energy portfolio while diversifying beyond solar and wind toward baseload geothermal generation.

energy-climate.title: Omani company to build 500 MW of solar in Botswana
energy-climate.summary: Oman's Naqaa Sustainable Energy LLC is set to design, finance, construct and operate a 500 MW solar project in northwestern Botswana. This major infrastructure development represents significant renewable energy investment in southern Africa and demonstrates international renewable energy capital deployment.

asia-pacific.title: Tai Po probe sees ideal progress but not yet time for Commission of Inquiry: chair
asia-pacific.summary:
This story has been made freely available as a public service to our readers. Please consider supporting SCMP’s journalism by subscribing.
A judge-led panel investigating the causes of Hong Kong’s deadliest fire in decades, which killed 168 people in Tai Po, will begin its 12th day of evidential hearings on Wednesday.
Representatives from the Fire Services Department and police are expected to testify before the independent committee tasked with investigating the causes of the Wang Fuk Court...

asia-pacific.title: US military says 4 killed in fourth ‘drug boat’ strike in a week
asia-pacific.summary:
The US military said on Tuesday that four people had been killed in a strike on another alleged drug-trafficking boat, the fourth such deadly attack in as many days.
US Southern Command (SOUTHCOM) said in a post on X that the strike – just as the previous three – occurred in the eastern Pacific Ocean. It posted aerial video on social media showing a vessel bobbing in the water before being struck by a projectile and exploding.
The four new fatalities raise the total death toll of the...

asia-pacific.title: Malaysian police recall overflowing cash, gems in 1MDB raid on Najib-linked condo
asia-pacific.summary:
Jewellery and cash worth hundreds of millions of ringgit were found in overfilled luggage bags stored in a luxury condominium in Kuala Lumpur during a raid linked to the 1Malaysia Develop­ment Bhd (1MDB) investigation, the High Court heard.
The police had found 71 ­luggage bags, a plastic box and more than 200 boxes during the operation at Pavilion Residences on May 17, 2018.
Former Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) director Amar Singh Ishar Singh testified that the sheer amount..

asia-pacific.title: Zhang Kai: why I left Yale for China at the peak of my career in life science
asia-pacific.summary:
Not all scientists are willing to share why they returned to China. Most remain tight-lipped about their reasons for returning home and their specific experiences because of political or other non-scientific reasons.
Professor Zhang Kai is an exception.
Zhang, who worked for many years in Cambridge, England, and Yale University in the United States, is one of the world’s top and most influential cryo-electron microscopy (cryo-EM) scientists while harbouring further ambitious goals.
“The first...

asia-pacific.title: Jailed witness reveals Duterte plotted to oust Philippines’ Marcos
asia-pacific.summary:
Former Philippine leader Rodrigo Duterte discussed removing President Ferdinand Marcos Jnr from office and even considered assassination if a supposed power-sharing deal fell apart, a jailed witness told a House impeachment hearing into Vice-President Sara Duterte-Carpio.
Ramil Madriaga, a self-described political organiser, intelligence operative and bagman who said he had once been close to Duterte-Carpio, made the sensational allegations before the House Committee on Justice on Tuesday during

asia-pacific.title: KMT chief shows way forward to avoid blind confrontation with Beijing
asia-pacific.summary:
Taiwan’s political pendulum is reversing. The government led by Democratic Progressive Party’s (DPP) William Lai Ching-te is increasingly discredited, not least for selling out the jewel of its semiconductor industry to the United States under pressure from President Donald Trump.
The Legislative Yuan is controlled by the opposition led by the Kuomintang (KMT). Party politics is now institutionalised into a sustained confrontation between the island’s presidency and its legislature. Now, budgets

asia-pacific.title: China AI robot restaurant analyses diners’ faces, tongues to recommend health-focused dishes
asia-pacific.summary:
Several restaurants in eastern China are using artificial intelligence (AI) robots to cook as many as 100 dishes to cut costs, sparking a heated discussion on social media.
In Hangzhou, Zhejiang province, at least three such eateries have been operating for months, Zhejiang TV reported.
One of them, 24 Jieqi Robot Restaurant in the Xihu district uses eight robots which handle ordering, serving, cleaning and cooking. They have shared 60 per cent of the total workload of kitchen workers, the...

asia-pacific.title: Call to check tech trends when drafting policies after basketball betting U-turn
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong authorities should consider the impact of technological developments when drafting policies, observers have said, noting the government’s decision to halt plans for basketball betting reflected a lack of thorough discussion.
Their remarks on Tuesday followed the government announcing the suspension over concerns about new “prediction market” gambling while warning that it was illegal for Hongkongers to bet on sports events on such trading platforms.
An amendment bill to the Betting...

asia-pacific.title: How PayMe’s 3.3 million users and Hong Kong firms could start using stablecoins
asia-pacific.summary:
For Hong Kong’s 3.3 million PayMe users, everyday transactions such as buying coffee or splitting bills with friends could soon involve stablecoins, with HSBC preparing to introduce the digital currency in the second half of this year.
That comes after HSBC Holdings, together with a Standard Chartered-led joint venture, became the first to obtain a stablecoin issuer licence from the Hong Kong Monetary Authority on Friday, marking a breakthrough in the city’s digital finance development.
HSBC,...

asia-pacific.title: Japan’s Narita Airport expansion revives row over forced land sales
asia-pacific.summary:
The operator of Japan’s Narita International Airport is considering compulsory acquisition of farmers’ land to complete a planned third runway, recalling the forced land purchases that led to violent clashes between riot police and local residents when the airport was built in the 1960s.
Narita International Airport Corp (NAA) has been able to secure 88.4 per cent of the land it requires to finish the extension of the existing 2,500-metre second runway at the airport and construct a new...
